name: Coconut Video Encoding API
what_it_does: Cloud video encoding API — enabling developers to transcode video files to multiple formats (MP4, WebM, HLS, MPEG-DASH) with automatic format detection, thumbnail generation, metadata extraction, and delivery to S3, GCS, Azure, FTP, or HTTP callbacks, with simple JSON job configuration and webhook notifications on completion.
use_cases: Encoding uploaded video files to multiple formats via Coconut API for multi-device delivery
Generating HLS and MPEG-DASH adaptive streams via Coconut API for video player compatibility
Creating video thumbnails and preview images via Coconut API for media application UX
Extracting video metadata via Coconut API for content management and cataloging
Processing bulk video archives via Coconut API for media library transcoding pipelines
not_for: Live video streaming (Coconut is VOD encoding only — use Wowza or AWS IVS for live)
Real-time video processing requiring sub-second latency
Teams needing video hosting and delivery CDN (Coconut is encode-only — use Mux or Cloudflare Stream)
best_when: A developer or SaaS platform needs a simple API to transcode user-uploaded video files to multiple formats and resolutions — with JSON job configuration, S3/GCS output, and webhook completion notifications.
avoid_when: You need live streaming, video hosting with CDN delivery, advanced DRM, or a full video platform (use Mux or Cloudflare Stream for full-stack video).

auth.notes: API key from Coconut account. HTTP Basic auth with API key as username. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes — API key provides full account access. Webhook (HTTP callback) on job completion with configurable URL. SDK for Ruby, PHP, Node.js, Python. Job-based API model — submit job, get status via poll or webhook. No real-time streaming API.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starter $9/month (1,500 min); Pro $29/month (5,000 min); Business $99/month (20,000 min); Pay-per-minute add-ons
pricing.notes: French company (Paris). Founded 2012 by Julien Moutte. Bootstrapped and profitable. Simple and affordable video encoding API. 500 free encoding minutes/month (competitive free tier). Multi-output encoding in single job (encode once to multiple formats). Outputs: MP4, WebM, OGG, HLS, MPEG-DASH, GIF. Input: URL, S3, GCS, Dropbox, HTTP. Output destinations: S3, GCS, Azure Blob, FTP, SFTP, HTTP. Thumbnail and sprite generation. SDK for Ruby, PHP, Node.js, Python. Popular with SaaS platforms and content sites for user video uploads.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with job-based model. HTTP Basic auth with API key. Webhook on job completion. Good documentation. SDKs for Ruby, PHP, Node.js, Python. GDPR compliant. Free 500 minutes/month. Encoding latency is asynchronous (job-based) — not real-time API. Concurrent job limits by plan.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Job status reads fully idempotent by job ID. Job creation not idempotent — same source creates new job each time. Output files idempotent if same destination path used (overwrites). Webhook delivery may retry on failure.
known_agent_gotchas: HTTP Basic auth with API key as username — non-standard auth pattern
Async job model — poll for status or use webhook; not real-time response
Concurrent job limits by plan — queue multiple jobs carefully
Encoding minutes counted from input duration — HD video uses more minutes
Output storage is YOUR S3/GCS/FTP — Coconut doesn't host encoded files

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. HTTP Basic auth with API key — use HTTPS only. Video files processed through Coconut infrastructure — evaluate for sensitive content. Output to user-controlled storage (S3/GCS). French company with GDPR compliance. No OAuth2 or scope granularity. Webhook URLs should use HTTPS.
